name: AISuite
pricing_notes: Fully open source (MIT); pay for underlying LLM provider API calls

auth.notes: Configure each provider's API key in environment; AISuite passes through to providers

performance.notes: Zero overhead — thin wrapper that passes calls through to provider SDKs

agent_readiness.notes: Andrew Ng's lightweight Python wrapper for querying multiple LLM providers with unified API. OpenAI-like interface. Supports Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Mistral, AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI, and more. Zero-dependency design — just thin provider adapters. Perfect for quick provider comparison or model switching in research code.

gotchas: No streaming in initial releases (added later for some providers)
Fewer features than LiteLLM (no caching, rate limiting, or load balancing)
Minimal error handling — exceptions bubble up from provider SDKs
New project — fewer integrations than mature alternatives
best_when: Quick multi-provider LLM comparison scripts without heavy framework setup
Research code needing easy model switching across providers
Teaching/tutorial contexts (simple, clean API for learning)
Agents needing lightweight provider abstraction without LiteLLM complexity
avoid_when: Production LLM routing needing fallbacks, caching, rate limits (use LiteLLM)
Teams needing comprehensive provider support with reliability features
Long-term production use where project maturity matters
summary: AISuite is Andrew Ng's lightweight Python library for querying multiple LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Groq, Mistral, Bedrock) with a unified OpenAI-compatible interface. Zero-dependency thin wrapper. Best for research, tutorials, and quick provider comparison scripts. For production routing with fallbacks, caching, and rate limiting, use LiteLLM instead.
